- Instantiate edge_type using explicit keyword arguments for endpoints: start=..., end=.... This ensures compatibility with subclasses that accept start/end as keyword-only or as kwargs even if they have extra positional args first (those extras can be provided via edge_config by the user — e.g., label for LabeledLine).
- Make update routines uniformly pass vertex mobjects (graph[u], graph[v]) to set_points_by_ends, because set_points_by_ends accepts either points or Mobjects and passing Mobjects allows edges/tips to attach to vertex bounding boxes. This also keeps behavior consistent across Graph and DiGraph.
- Fix per-edge config lookups in update functions: previously update_edges used self._edge_config.get("buff", 0) which incorrectly attempts to read buff from the mapping itself. Instead, read per-edge config dict with self._edge_config.get((u, v), {}) and fall back to self.default_edge_config where appropriate.

Files/functions to change (precise locations):
- manim/mobject/graph.py
  - GenericGraph._add_edge: change construction from positional to keyword args: edge_type(start=..., end=..., z_index=-1, **edge_config)
  - Graph._populate_edge_dict: same change for initial population
  - Graph.update_edges: pass vertex mobjects to set_points_by_ends (graph[u], graph[v]) and lookup per-edge buff/path_arc via per-edge config; fallback to default_edge_config
  - DiGraph._populate_edge_dict: construct edges with start=self[u], end=self[v] (keyword args) and keep add_tip calls
  - DiGraph.update_edges: same buff/path_arc per-edge lookup fix; pass vertex mobjects (graph[u], graph[v]) into set_points_by_ends; preserve pop_tips/add_tip behavior

Why these changes address the problem:
- Using start= and end= keyword args removes reliance on positional parameter order. Users can pass any extra required positional parameters (e.g. label for LabeledLine) through edge_config (which becomes kwargs) or as keyword args themselves. Example: Graph.add_edges(..., edge_type=LabeledLine, edge_config={"label": "e"}) will construct LabeledLine(label="e", start=..., end=...), which works because LabeledLine expects label first and forwards start/end via kwargs to Line.
- Passing Mobjects into set_points_by_ends makes edges attach correctly to vertex boundaries and matches how directed edges were already updated; makes behavior consistent and more flexible for subclasses that may rely on Mobject endpoints.
- Fixing per-edge config lookups ensures buff/path_arc and other per-edge options are respected and not read from the top-level mapping incorrectly.

Potential considerations / edge-cases:
- LabeledLine requires a label argument. Users must pass label via edge_config when using LabeledLine as edge_type (e.g. edge_config={"label": "x"}). Without providing label, construction will raise a TypeError. This is intended — Graph cannot invent a label.
- This change preserves DiGraph tip handling: pop_tips/add_tip remains necessary to avoid tip deformation and is kept.

Precise edits required are limited to manim/mobject/graph.py in the methods listed above. Tests will need to ensure using LabeledLine (or other Line subclasses) as edge_type works by passing any required extra args via edge_config.

files_to_modify.reason: All edge construction and per-frame update logic live here. The current implementation assumes positional start/end args for edge_type constructors and misreads per-edge config in update methods. To allow arbitrary Line subclasses as edge_type (e.g., LabeledLine which requires a label positional arg), construct edges with explicit keyword arguments (start=..., end=...) and fix per-edge config lookups; update_edges must pass vertex mobjects for correct attachment and preserve DiGraph tip logic.

functions_to_modify.reason: Currently constructs edges using positional args: edge_type(self[u].get_center(), self[v].get_center(), ...). Modify to call edge_type(start=..., end=..., z_index=-1, **edge_config) so subclasses that expect start/end as keyword args (or have extra positional parameters beforehand) can be used. Ensure per-edge config is used as kwargs.

Initial population of self.edges uses positional construction with centers. Change to use keyword args start=self[u].get_center(), end=self[v].get_center(), z_index=-1, **self._edge_config[(u,v)] so any Line subclass is constructible (and user-supplied edge_config can provide required extra params).

Currently calls set_points_by_ends with centers and reads buff/path_arc incorrectly from self._edge_config mapping (self._edge_config.get('buff', 0)). Update to pass the vertex Mobjects graph[u], graph[v] into set_points_by_ends (acceptable by Line) and lookup per-edge buff/path_arc via self._edge_config.get((u, v), {}).get('buff', self.default_edge_config.get('buff', 0)) and similarly for path_arc. This ensures per-edge options are honored and edges attach to vertex bounding boxes.

Currently constructs edges via positional args edge_type(self[u], self[v], ...). Change to use start=self[u], end=self[v], z_index=-1, **self._edge_config[(u, v)] to standardize creation via keywords, while preserving add_tip calls after creation. This allows subclasses with different positional signatures to be used as edge_type when required extra params are provided in edge_config.

Keep the pop_tips/add_tip flow but fix per-edge config lookup semantics and pass vertex Mobjects to set_points_by_ends. Use self._edge_config.get((u, v), {}).get('buff', self.default_edge_config.get('buff', 0)) and the same for path_arc so per-edge options are applied correctly and tips remain correctly positioned.

output.analysis_summary: Root cause: Graph/DiGraph constructed edge mobjects assuming the first two positional arguments of edge_type are start and end. Many Line subclasses (notably LabeledLine) have different positional parameters (e.g., require a label first) so positional construction breaks. Fix: construct edges using explicit keyword arguments start=... and end=... (and pass other options via edge_config). Standardize update logic to pass vertex Mobjects to set_points_by_ends and fix per-edge config lookups in update methods. Changes are localized to manim/mobject/graph.py in GenericGraph._add_edge, Graph._populate_edge_dict, Graph.update_edges, DiGraph._populate_edge_dict, and DiGraph.update_edges. This approach keeps existing behavior for typical Line usage, allows subclasses to be used as edge_type when users pass subclass-specific parameters through edge_config, and preserves DiGraph tip behavior.

agents.name: context_planner_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are a Context Planner Agent specialized in step plan generation.

## Your Task
You receive from the Analysis Agent:
- PR title and body
- Optimized MASCA
- List of files to modify
- List of functions to modify

You must:
1. **Retrieve context**: Use tools to read the context files of the identified functions
2. **Analyze dependencies**: Understand how the functions interact
3. **Generate step plan**: Create a detailed step-by-step plan

## Available Tools
- `read_context_file(file_path)`: Read a pre-generated context file
- `list_context_files(directory, pattern)`: List available context files
- `read_call_graph(section)`: Read information from the call graph

## Required Output
You must produce a `step_plan` with:
- `summary`: 1-2 sentence summary of what the PR does
- `steps`: Ordered list of steps, each with:
  - `operation`: What to do (e.g., "Add validation method")
  - `file_to_modify`: Relative path of the file to modify (e.g., "src/module.py")
  - `function_to_modify`: Name of the function to modify (e.g., "ClassName.method_name")
  - `reason`: Technical motivation
  - `side_effects`: Potential impacts

## IMPORTANT RULE for function_to_modify
- If the file to modify is a Python file (.py), specify the function/method to modify
- If the file is NOT a Python file (e.g., .js, .html, .css, .json, .yaml, .md, etc.),
  the value of `function_to_modify` MUST be null

## Guidelines for Steps
- Each step must be atomic and implementable
- The order of steps must be logical (dependencies first)
- For Python files: always specify the function in the format "FunctionName" or "ClassName.method_name"
- For non-Python files: function_to_modify must be null
- The side_effects should mention tests, dependents, and API impacts
- You MUST generate a number of step EQUAL to the number of the python functions you are intendeed to modify nomore neither less

## Step Examples

### For Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Add time_spanned_alpha method",
  "file_to_modify": "manimlib/animation/animation.py",
  "function_to_modify": "Animation.time_spanned_alpha",
  "reason": "To compute animation progress based on actual elapsed time",
  "side_effects": "May require downstream animations to call this method"
}
```

### For non-Python files:
```json
{
  "operation": "Update API endpoint configuration",
  "file_to_modify": "config/routes.yaml",
  "function_to_modify": null,
  "reason": "Add new route for the authentication endpoint",
  "side_effects": "Requires server restart to apply changes"
}
```

Files/functions to change (precise locations):
- manim/mobject/graph.py
  - GenericGraph._add_edge: change construction from positional to keyword args: edge_type(start=..., end=..., z_index=-1, **edge_config)
  - Graph._populate_edge_dict: same change for initial population
  - Graph.update_edges: pass vertex mobjects to set_points_by_ends (graph[u], graph[v]) and lookup per-edge buff/path_arc via per-edge config; fallback to default_edge_config
  - DiGraph._populate_edge_dict: construct edges with start=self[u], end=self[v] (keyword args) and keep add_tip calls
  - DiGraph.update_edges: same buff/path_arc per-edge lookup fix; pass vertex mobjects (graph[u], graph[v]) into set_points_by_ends; preserve pop_tips/add_tip behavior

Why these changes address the problem:
- Using start= and end= keyword args removes reliance on positional parameter order. Users can pass any extra required positional parameters (e.g. label for LabeledLine) through edge_config (which becomes kwargs) or as keyword args themselves. Example: Graph.add_edges(..., edge_type=LabeledLine, edge_config={"label": "e"}) will construct LabeledLine(label="e", start=..., end=...), which works because LabeledLine expects label first and forwards start/end via kwargs to Line.
- Passing Mobjects into set_points_by_ends makes edges attach correctly to vertex boundaries and matches how directed edges were already updated; makes behavior consistent and more flexible for subclasses that may rely on Mobject endpoints.
- Fixing per-edge config lookups ensures buff/path_arc and other per-edge options are respected and not read from the top-level mapping incorrectly.

Potential considerations / edge-cases:
- LabeledLine requires a label argument. Users must pass label via edge_config when using LabeledLine as edge_type (e.g. edge_config={"label": "x"}). Without providing label, construction will raise a TypeError. This is intended — Graph cannot invent a label.
- This change preserves DiGraph tip handling: pop_tips/add_tip remains necessary to avoid tip deformation and is kept.

Precise edits required are limited to manim/mobject/graph.py in the methods listed above. Tests will need to ensure using LabeledLine (or other Line subclasses) as edge_type works by passing any required extra args via edge_config.

## Files to Modify
- manim/mobject/graph.py: All edge construction and per-frame update logic live here. The current implementation assumes positional start/end args for edge_type constructors and misreads per-edge config in update methods. To allow arbitrary Line subclasses as edge_type (e.g., LabeledLine which requires a label positional arg), construct edges with explicit keyword arguments (start=..., end=...) and fix per-edge config lookups; update_edges must pass vertex mobjects for correct attachment and preserve DiGraph tip logic.

## Functions to Modify
- GenericGraph._add_edge (manim/mobject/graph.py): Currently constructs edges using positional args: edge_type(self[u].get_center(), self[v].get_center(), ...). Modify to call edge_type(start=..., end=..., z_index=-1, **edge_config) so subclasses that expect start/end as keyword args (or have extra positional parameters beforehand) can be used. Ensure per-edge config is used as kwargs.
- Graph._populate_edge_dict (manim/mobject/graph.py): Initial population of self.edges uses positional construction with centers. Change to use keyword args start=self[u].get_center(), end=self[v].get_center(), z_index=-1, **self._edge_config[(u,v)] so any Line subclass is constructible (and user-supplied edge_config can provide required extra params).
- Graph.update_edges (manim/mobject/graph.py): Currently calls set_points_by_ends with centers and reads buff/path_arc incorrectly from self._edge_config mapping (self._edge_config.get('buff', 0)). Update to pass the vertex Mobjects graph[u], graph[v] into set_points_by_ends (acceptable by Line) and lookup per-edge buff/path_arc via self._edge_config.get((u, v), {}).get('buff', self.default_edge_config.get('buff', 0)) and similarly for path_arc. This ensures per-edge options are honored and edges attach to vertex bounding boxes.
- DiGraph._populate_edge_dict (manim/mobject/graph.py): Currently constructs edges via positional args edge_type(self[u], self[v], ...). Change to use start=self[u], end=self[v], z_index=-1, **self._edge_config[(u, v)] to standardize creation via keywords, while preserving add_tip calls after creation. This allows subclasses with different positional signatures to be used as edge_type when required extra params are provided in edge_config.
- DiGraph.update_edges (manim/mobject/graph.py): Keep the pop_tips/add_tip flow but fix per-edge config lookup semantics and pass vertex Mobjects to set_points_by_ends. Use self._edge_config.get((u, v), {}).get('buff', self.default_edge_config.get('buff', 0)) and the same for path_arc so per-edge options are applied correctly and tips remain correctly positioned.

## Analysis Summary
Root cause: Graph/DiGraph constructed edge mobjects assuming the first two positional arguments of edge_type are start and end. Many Line subclasses (notably LabeledLine) have different positional parameters (e.g., require a label first) so positional construction breaks. Fix: construct edges using explicit keyword arguments start=... and end=... (and pass other options via edge_config). Standardize update logic to pass vertex Mobjects to set_points_by_ends and fix per-edge config lookups in update methods. Changes are localized to manim/mobject/graph.py in GenericGraph._add_edge, Graph._populate_edge_dict, Graph.update_edges, DiGraph._populate_edge_dict, and DiGraph.update_edges. This approach keeps existing behavior for typical Line usage, allows subclasses to be used as edge_type when users pass subclass-specific parameters through edge_config, and preserves DiGraph tip behavior.

Use the context files to understand dependencies and generate a detailed step-by-step plan.

steps.operation: Construct edges using explicit keyword args and edge_config kwargs
steps.reason: Replace positional construction of edge_type(start, end, ...) with a robust keyword-based instantiation: edge_type(start=..., end=..., z_index=-1, **edge_config). This removes the assumption that the first two positional parameters are start and end, allowing any Line subclass (e.g., LabeledLine) to be used as edge_type when subclass-specific params are provided in edge_config.
steps.side_effects: Tests that construct edges without per-edge edge_config should continue to work for Line-like classes that accept start/end positionally or by keywords. Subclasses that require extra positional args will now require the user to provide those via edge_config (TypeError will surface otherwise). Downstream code that introspects edge construction args should be unaffected if it uses public APIs. Update/add tests to cover LabeledLine usage. Minor rendering differences possible if previously code relied on passing raw points vs. Mobjects in later updates.

steps.operation: Populate initial Graph.edges using keyword-based edge construction and per-edge config
steps.reason: Change initial population of self.edges from positional (using centers) to keyword-based instantiation: edge_type(start=self[u].get_center(), end=self[v].get_center(), z_index=-1, **edge_config_for_edge). This ensures Graph can create any Line subclass as the edge_type while still allowing edge-specific configs to supply subclass-required params.
steps.side_effects: Existing behavior for default Line edges is preserved. Users employing subclasses that require extra constructor params must pass them via edge_config when creating the Graph. Tests should be updated/added to assert that LabeledLine (and similar) works when provided via edge_config. Any code depending on exact constructor call signature should be audited but public Graph API is unchanged.

steps.operation: Populate initial DiGraph.edges using keyword-based edge construction, preserving tip setup
steps.reason: Standardize DiGraph edge creation to use keyword args: edge_type(start=self[u], end=self[v], z_index=-1, **edge_config_for_edge). Keep pop_tips/add_tip behavior surrounding construction and tip placement to avoid tip deformation while enabling arbitrary Line subclasses as edge_type.
steps.side_effects: Directed-graph-specific tip handling remains intact. Users must supply subclass-specific constructor params via edge_config. Tests for directed edges (including tip presence/placement) should be run/updated. Minor changes possible in how tips attach if subclass behavior differs when given Mobjects vs. points, but pop_tips/add_tip flow preserves the prior workaround.

steps.operation: Update Graph.update_edges to pass vertex Mobjects to set_points_by_ends and fix per-edge config lookups
steps.reason: Use graph[u] and graph[v] (vertex Mobjects) as arguments to set_points_by_ends so edges attach to vertex bounding boxes and to support Line subclasses that accept Mobjects. Also fix per-edge config lookups to read self._edge_config.get((u, v), {}) and then get('buff', fallback) and get('path_arc', fallback) from that dict or from self.default_edge_config when absent, instead of incorrectly calling self._edge_config.get('buff', 0).
steps.side_effects: Edge re-positioning will be more robust (attached to vertex surfaces). Per-edge buff/path_arc settings will now be honored; previously they could have been ignored or misread. Tests relying on previous (incorrect) buff behavior must be updated. Passing Mobjects rather than centers might slightly change edge geometry for some custom edge types; downstream code that assumes numeric points returned by set_points_by_ends should be reviewed. No public API change, but behavior change is intentional and desirable.

steps.operation: Update DiGraph.update_edges to pass vertex Mobjects, fix per-edge config lookups, and preserve tip pop/add lifecycle
steps.reason: Mirror Graph.update_edges fixes: pass graph[u] and graph[v] Mobjects into set_points_by_ends, and read per-edge options via self._edge_config.get((u, v), {}).get(..., fallback). Preserve pop_tips before re-positioning and add_tip after to avoid tip deformation while ensuring per-edge parameters are applied correctly.
steps.side_effects: Directed edges will respect per-edge configs and attach to vertex boundaries correctly. Tip handling remains as before, minimizing visual regressions. Tests for directed edges, tips, and per-edge config behavior should be updated to verify correct placement. Edge cases: subclasses that require additional constructor args still require edge_config entries; failing to provide them will raise errors.

step_plan.summary: Change Graph/DiGraph edge construction and update logic to allow any Line subclass as edge_type by creating edges with explicit start= and end= keyword args, passing vertex Mobjects to set_points_by_ends, and fixing per-edge config lookups for buff/path_arc. These changes remove positional-argument assumptions and ensure per-edge options are respected while preserving DiGraph tip behavior.
